hero.title: 3 Days in Amritsar.
hero.titleAccent: The Golden Temple and a City With a Big Heart.
hero.intro: Some places stay with you. Amritsar is one of them. The Golden Temple at first light, langar served free to thousands, the roar of the Wagah border crowd each evening. This is Punjab at its warmest.

breadcrumb.label: 3 Days Amritsar: Golden Temple & Punjabi Heritage

hero.heroImageAlt: Golden Temple Amritsar at dawn reflected in the sacred pool

overview.heading: 3 Days Amritsar: Golden Temple & Punjabi Heritage: The Full Picture
body: Amritsar is the spiritual home of Sikhism, and the Golden Temple sits at its centre. Officially Harmandir Sahib, it is one of the most moving places of worship anywhere. Gold dome, white marble, a vast reflecting pool, and a free community kitchen that feeds up to 100,000 people a day. You do not have to be religious to feel something here.

This 3 day Amritsar tour package from Malaysia keeps it simple and personal. You travel privately with your own vehicle and driver guide, so you visit the temple at the times that suit you. Early morning and after dark are the most beautiful, when the gold glows against the sky.

Beyond the temple, Amritsar holds the sobering Jallianwala Bagh memorial, the legendary food of the old city, and the daily Wagah border ceremony. Three days is enough to take it all in without rushing, and to eat extremely well along the way.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: The Golden Temple at Dawn and After Dark
items.body: Harmandir Sahib is the heart of Amritsar. Go early for stillness, or after sunset when the gilded dome glows over the Amrit Sarovar. Walk the parikrama, listen to live kirtan, and if you wish, join the langar where a free meal is served to all.

items.title: The Wagah Border Ceremony
items.body: Every evening at the India-Pakistan border, soldiers perform a high-energy flag-lowering ritual. The stamping, the crowds, the patriotic songs. Theatrical and unforgettable. Your guide handles the timing for a good seat.

items.title: Jallianwala Bagh & the Old City
items.body: A short walk from the temple, Jallianwala Bagh remembers the 1919 massacre. A quiet, important place. Around it, narrow lanes hide some of Punjab's best food, from kulchas to lassi in heavy clay cups.

items.title: Punjabi Food You Will Not Forget
items.body: Amritsari kulcha, makki di roti with sarson da saag, butter-laden dal, and lassi thick enough to stand a spoon in. Your guide steers you to legendary local spots, not tourist traps.

days.title: Arrive Amritsar
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. The evening is free. A first visit to the Golden Temple after dark, lit up and reflected in the pool, is the perfect introduction.
suggestedActivities: Evening visit to the illuminated Golden Temple
Lassi and kulcha near the temple

days.title: Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh & Wagah Border
days.description: Your private vehicle and guide are with you all day. Morning at the Golden Temple and Jallianwala Bagh, then an afternoon drive to Wagah for the evening ceremony.

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. Shop for Punjabi juttis, phulkari textiles or dried fruits, or pay one last visit to the temple before your airport transfer.

sections.heading: Amritsar Tour Package from Malaysia: What to Expect
sections.body:
Amritsar is one of the most rewarding short trips in North India, and it is genuinely easy from Malaysia. The Golden Temple is the obvious draw, but the city's food, history and border spectacle round out a full three days.

This package is private throughout. No coach, no fixed group. Your driver guide knows the best times to visit Harmandir Sahib and how to navigate the old city, which matters because the lanes are narrow and crowds are real.

Amritsar pairs beautifully with other North India spots. Many travellers add it to a Golden Triangle trip or extend into the hills at Dharamshala.

sections.heading: Visiting the Golden Temple: Practical Notes
sections.body:
Everyone is welcome, regardless of religion, and there is no entry fee. You cover your head (scarves are at the entrance) and remove your shoes, stored free.

The langar, the free community kitchen, is one of the most remarkable things you will ever see. Volunteers serve a simple vegetarian meal to anyone who sits down.

Before 7am and after the temple is lit are the most atmospheric times. Your guide plans around these windows.

sections.heading: Is Three Days in Amritsar Enough?
sections.body:
For the Golden Temple, Jallianwala Bagh, Wagah and a proper round of Punjabi food, three days is comfortable.

If you want more, Amritsar combines naturally with Dharamshala and McLeod Ganj, which is what our 6 day Amritsar and Dharamsala package covers. It also folds neatly into a 7 day Golden Triangle plus Amritsar route.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Sri Guru Ram Dass Jee International Airport, Amritsar (ATQ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Amritsar?
items.answer: October to March is the most pleasant, with cool days and cold December-January nights. April to June is very hot. July to September brings the monsoon. The Golden Temple is beautiful year-round, but winter suits sightseeing and Wagah best.

items.question: Do I cover my head at the Golden Temple?
items.answer: Yes, both men and women cover their heads inside the complex. Scarves are available at the entrance, or bring your own. Shoes are removed and kept at a free counter. Dress modestly.

items.question: Is the langar meal really free?
items.answer: Yes. The langar serves a free vegetarian meal to everyone, every day, run by volunteers and donations. Visitors can eat, and many also volunteer to help serve or wash dishes.

items.question: Can I combine Amritsar with Dharamshala or the Golden Triangle?
items.answer: Yes. Dharamshala is around 5 to 6 hours by road, covered in our 6 day Amritsar plus Dharamsala package. Amritsar also pairs with Delhi, Agra and Jaipur in a 7 day Golden Triangle plus Amritsar itinerary.

items.question: Is Amritsar suitable for elderly travellers?
items.answer: Yes. The temple complex has ramps and is largely accessible, and the private vehicle means minimal walking between sights. Wagah involves some stairs and standing, so we advise on the best seating.
